mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series grow > 2% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, causing immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of tightening-cycle contractions, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.8% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and supports corporate revenue projections, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal reverses direction when monthly additions fall below 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer leverage that precedes a demand correction, triggering sharp short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during divergence episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output alongside stable employment during a post-shock recovery phase signals productive capacity expansion that boosts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal fails to predict returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% quarterly.
